inputs.name: competitors
inputs.description: Competitor websites to monitor, one per line

inputs.name: track_pricing
inputs.description: Monitor competitor pricing pages for changes

inputs.name: track_features
inputs.description: Monitor competitor feature pages for changes

competitor-scan.description: Monitor competitor websites for meaningful changes and emit alerts

activities.intent: Scrape competitor websites and detect meaningful changes
steps: Retrieve the list of tracked competitors and their monitored URLs
Scrape each competitor's key pages: pricing, features, homepage, blog
Compare current content against the last saved snapshot
Filter out cosmetic changes — only flag pricing shifts, new features, messaging pivots, or team changes

activities.intent: Evaluate whether detected changes are strategically meaningful
steps: For each detected change, assess strategic impact on the user's competitive position
Categorize as high, medium, or low impact
For high-impact changes, draft a brief with context, impact, and recommended response
Only emit competitor.change event if at least one high or medium impact change is found

battlecard-update.description: Monthly refresh of competitive battlecards with latest positioning and features

activities.intent: Gather the latest information on each tracked competitor
steps: For each tracked competitor, research current pricing, features, positioning, and recent announcements
Check review sites, social media, and industry publications for sentiment and feedback
Identify any new strengths or weaknesses since the last battlecard update

activities.intent: Produce updated one-page battlecards for each competitor
steps: For each competitor, produce a one-page battlecard: their pitch, their strengths, their weaknesses, your counter-positioning, and proof points
Highlight what changed since last month
Include talk tracks for sales conversations
Save updated battlecards and note the refresh date

win-loss-analysis.description: Structured analysis of why deals were won or lost against specific competitors

activities.intent: Collect and structure the deal information for analysis
steps: Ask the user for deal details: competitor, outcome (won/lost), deal size, buyer persona, and key moments
If available, review any notes, emails, or call transcripts from the deal
Structure the information into a standard win/loss template

activities.intent: Identify root causes and produce actionable recommendations
steps: Identify the primary reason the deal was won or lost
Categorize into themes: pricing, features, trust, timing, relationships, or positioning
Compare against historical win/loss patterns with this competitor
Produce 2-3 specific recommendations to improve win rate against this competitor
Update the competitor's battlecard if the analysis reveals new insights
